title: Fever Dream Bird
about:
The Fever Dream Bird, also known as the Afterlife Bird, is a nickname for a bird seen in the 1973 animated short film The Galaxy (Galaxia) by Sabin Bălașa, which was coined on TikTok in late 2025 from a series of viral video caption memes that likened the bird to a creature one might see in an intense fever dream. The bird is seen spreading its wings, which eventually take up the whole background, turning to look at the viewer and then flying upward as its figure obscures.

Reminiscent of the "Ever Dream This Man?" meme, Fever Dream Bird memes played on the eerie and mesmerizing nature of the animation and hailed it as a cryptid shared through collective hallucinations, similar to The Hatman. Some also gave meaning to encountering the bird in a dream.
origin:
On May 2nd, 2025, Instagram [1] user @real.young.man shared a clip from the 1973 animated short film The Galaxy by Sabin Bălașa,[2] which showed a bird spreading its wings, looking at the viewer and flying upward. The Reel received over 297,400 likes in seven months.

The full film was shared on YouTube[3] by the channel Sabin Balasa Animation Films on May 12th, 2018, gaining over 584,600 views and 34,000 likes in seven years. The bird scene occurs at the film's 6:48 timestamp.[4]

On November 22nd, 2025, TikToker[5] @mayjay_15 shared an animation meme, showing a character unable to sleep with a text overlay caption reading, "I experience the fever dream bird when I was sick, maybe like 5 years old or something." The character sees the bird from The Galaxy in the video. Over 11 days, the video received over 341,400 likes.

The video is currently the first known reference to the bird as a "fever dream" bird. However, top comments left on Instagram[1] user @real.young.man's video joked about, "Am I going to die in 7 days now or something," and "King Von's final moments," months before.
